q: In which city were the first modern Olympic Games held in 1896?
exp: They were revived by Pierre de Coubertin in Greece.

q: Which tennis player holds the record for most men's Grand Slam singles titles (to 2024)?
exp: Djokovic passed 24 Grand Slam singles titles.

q: Which is the only country to play in every football World Cup finals?
exp: Brazil has appeared in every tournament since 1930.

q: Who holds the men's 100 m world record?
exp: Bolt ran 9.58 s in Berlin in 2009.

q: Which trophy is the oldest in international sport still contested?
Which gymnast has several elements named after her for being a pioneer?
exp: Biles has multiple skills named after her in the code of points.

q: How many points is a three-pointer worth in basketball?
exp: The NBA introduced the three-point line in 1979.

q: Which Formula 1 driver won seven World Championships, tying the record?
exp: Hamilton and Michael Schumacher each have seven titles.

q: Which country has hosted the Summer Olympics the most times?
exp: The USA has hosted the Summer Games four times (with LA 2028 making five).

q: Which boxer was known as 'The Greatest' and lit the 1996 Olympic flame?
exp: Ali lit the cauldron at the Atlanta 1996 Games.

q: In cricket, how many players are in a team?
exp: Like football, a cricket team fields 11 players.

q: Which Portuguese club first won the European Cup, in 1961?
exp: Benfica won it in 1961 and 1962 with Eusébio in the side.
